IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PROMOTE IDEAS AND SOLUTIONS FOR PEACE AND SECURITY IN THE UNITED STATES AND ISRAEL THROUGH SCHOLARLY ANALYSIS AND CRITICAL ASSESSMENT OF MIDDLE EAST ISSUES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: PROMOTE IDEAS AND SOLUTIONS THROUGH SCHOLARLY ANALYSIS, CRITICAL ASSESSMENT OF MIDDLE EAST PEACE, SECURITY CONFRONTING THE USA AND ISRAEL
IRS990.Desc: EMET hosts seminars, debates, and educational forums featuring Middle East experts to educate the public and policy makers on the common threats facing Israel and the USA. EMET also publishes articles and participates in other forums to share research.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part II minor adjustments made for previous years contributions as per 2016 and 2017 audit as comple
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: Part II minor adjustments made for previous years contributions as per 2016 and 2017 audit as completed after the 2017 990 deadline.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Allocation of Website to organize supporters to sign petitions in favor or against proposed congressional legislation.
Audit report in GAAP basis reports dinner expenses as an expense instead a deduction from income. Tax basis reports dinner expenses as a deduction from income.
Audit report in GAAP basis reports dinner expenses as an expense instead a deduction from income. Tax basis reports dinner expenses as a deduction from income.

Regarding receivable due from Sarah Stern. The qualified parking benefit became subject to UBIT tax as part of the TCJA. 501c3 organizations had until March 31, 2019 to make changes in benefits and accounting in order to not be subject to UBIT for qualified transportion benefits. Hence a receivable was established from Sarah Stern, Director and President, to EMET and was repaid before March 31, 2019. Regarding business transaction with Eytan Laor. Mr. Laor is a Board Member who is also an owner of a Electoral Strategies Inc that specilizes in fundraising . He has a contract for percentage of certain new donor fundraising money and was paid $15,000 in 2018.
BOD members Karen and Philip Cohen, Ed and Jill Gerstenfield, Alan and Barbara Kruglak, are all spouses.
Copy of the 990 provided to officers of the BOD before filing.
Organization sets pay subject consistent with like organiations and subject to budget constraints.
Organization sets pay subject consistent with like organiations and subject to budget constraints.
Organizing documents and form 990 are available upon request.
Annual registrations outsourced (management). Social Media (program column).

2017 Audit Completed after the filing of the 2017 990 resulted in a change of $2500 in fund balance attributed to deferred income of $2500 reported on 2017 balance sheet (Part X, line 19, column A) which was reclassified as a temporarily restricted asset in the 2017 audit. Correction results in a $2500 fund balance difference. $2 of other changes attributed to rounding differences.
